title: Specs ledger: repo markdown is scanned, deterministically graded, and surfaced through the wiki as the trusted reading layer
context: Owner requirement: spec/docs markdown must flow into the wiki, Hunch must inspect + heal them, and once external docs go stale the wiki alone should be a sufficient reading surface. The graph stays the only source of truth — so the wiki cannot copy prose; it must GRADE it.
decision: src/core/docscan.ts scans all repo markdown (same bounded walk hunch drift uses; generated hunch:wiki pages excluded) and grades each doc deterministically: GROUNDED (topic anchors resolving to current decisions), STALE (pin superseded/dangling, proposed-but-shipped marker, or every src/*.ts reference missing), UNVERIFIED (no anchors, no signals — the honest tier). The wiki renders this as wiki/specs.md — a ledger page: stale docs carry their exact issues + heal guidance ('read the component pages instead'), unverified docs get anchoring instructions; component pages gain a 'Docs & specs' section (docs associated by the src files they mention, badge per grade; stale ones marked do-not-trust). Freshness: the ledger is hashed over the doc-STATUS snapshot (rel/title/status/issues/topics — not raw prose, so cosmetic edits don't churn), stored under the reserved '_specs' manifest id (never orphan-removed); grade changes fire the existing wiki-stale drift and hunch wiki --heal regenerates only what moved. Doc statuses also ride each component pack's hash, so a spec going stale re-renders the pages that cite it. Prose is never rewritten — healing a stale doc stays a human edit guided by hunch heal.
consequences: The wiki README links the ledger with grounded/stale/unverified counts — the 'can I trust the docs?' answer is one click from the index
Docs with only illustrative code paths (e.g. README marketing examples) grade stale until anchored — aggressive but honest; anchoring or real paths fixes it
drift.ts now imports the markdown walk from docscan.ts (single walker for drift checks and grading)
Private home grades docs against the FULL decision union, so an overlay decision can ground a doc in the private wiki that looks unverified publicly
alternatives_rejected: Copying doc prose into wiki pages: creates a second editable truth and stales instantly; the ledger grades and routes instead
LLM-judged doc freshness: semantic guessing violates the deterministic-drift philosophy; only explicit pins, status markers, and resolvable code refs may grade
Hashing raw doc content into the ledger: any typo fix would churn the wiki; the status snapshot is the meaningful input
A separate drift kind for doc grades: wiki-stale already models 'a generated page's inputs moved'; one heal verb (hunch wiki --heal) covers it
